_comment: Configurable display copy for Just a Little Fun. Edit freely. Tokens in {curly braces} are filled in at runtime. Available tokens are noted per section. Host reads this from ./data/phrases.json; the player page reads the same file. NOTE: the announcer 'milestones' and 'raceCalls' sections were removed — those calls are now voice-only and their variety lives in the announcer voice-line manifest (vo-manifest.json), not here. Any field whose value is an ARRAY is a pool of interchangeable variants: the code picks one at random each time, so repeated events feel fresh. Single-string fields are used verbatim.
wipeTransitions: It's a great day for racing!

champion._tokens: {n} = number of punters, {amount} = dollar figure (no sign). Each field is an array of variants chosen at random.
subMany: The sharpest eye on the card
profitDown: ▼ Lost ${amount} and still won the room
profitFlat: Square on the night — and still on top
Broke even and took the crown
Not a dollar moved — still the best
Level pegging, top of the pile

placement._comment: Player-side end-of-game placement copy. rank 1/2/3 and last are special; middle-of-field scales by band (top/mid/back). 'medal' is a single emoji; 'headline' and the 'sub' fields are arrays of variants chosen at random.

headline: Champion punter!
You took the room!
Top of the tote!
Winner, winner!
subMany: You topped the tote and took the whole room. Drinks are on you.
Nobody backed them better. The room is yours.
Sharpest eyes on the night — you cleaned up.
First across the line where it counts. Take a bow.
subSolo: Last one standing — the title is yours.
You outlasted every other punter. Champion by attrition.
The lone survivor takes the crown.

headline: So, so close
Pipped at the post
A whisker short
Second by a nose
sub: Pipped right at the post. A whisker off the top spot — unlucky.
A hair away from the crown. Cruel margins.
So near the top you could taste it. Next card's yours.
Beaten by a whisker — hold your head high.

headline: On the podium
Bronze and proud
A place on the dais
Third and thereabouts
sub: A spot on the dais and bragging rights to match. Solid card.
You made the podium — no small feat in this room.
Third place and plenty to be pleased about.
On the board and in the money. Nicely done.

headline: Wooden spoon
Someone had to
The cellar's yours
Down the very back
sub: Someone had to prop up the field — might as well wear it with pride. There's always next card.
Rock bottom, but you were in it till the end. Chin up.
The wooden spoon is a badge of sorts. Wear it well.
Last of the pack — nowhere to go but up next time.

headline: Top of the chasers
Just off the medals
Best of the rest
Knocking on the podium
sub: Just missed the medals but well clear of the pack. Nicely played.
A place or two off the dais — a strong card all the same.
Best of the rest tonight. The podium was in sight.
So close to a medal. Well clear of the muddle.

headline: Mid-field finish
Right in the mix
Smack in the middle
Held your own
sub: Right in the thick of it — a few bets away from the top end. Respectable night.
Squarely mid-field. A tidy result with room to climb.
In the thick of the pack all night. Solid enough.
Middle of the road — a few good calls from the sharp end.

headline: Down the field
Near the back
Dodged the spoon
A tough card
sub: Hung in there near the back, but you dodged the wooden spoon. We'll call that a win.
Back of the pack, but not the very bottom. Small mercies.
A rough night at the windows — but you avoided the spoon.
Near the rear, still swinging. Next card's a fresh start.
